summary: BA iter1 resolved OBJ-1 cleanly (AC-D3 now correctly enumerates 6 production callers with line citations; non-callers labeled NOT in target list; component_chain and context JSON aligned). However, OBJ-2 and OBJ-3 are only TEXTUALLY ADDRESSED — the revised spec text introduces three new internal contradictions/defects that block PASS, all surfaced by codex iter2 adversarial review and verified by direct file-read: (NEW-1, was OBJ-2 residual) AC-H3 GIVEN clause (line 241) lists 'missing subagent_type in stdin' as an orphan-triggering precondition with general THEN (line 244) 'subagent_calls UNCHANGED', but Technical Hints Case B (line 286) explicitly says when subagent_type is absent, the legacy chain MAY write subagent_calls if it resolves a valid in_progress step with subagent_call metadata — this is exactly AC-H2's serial-dispatch case. AC-H3 line 246 partially qualifies the orphan condition (legacy returns None) but does not remove the contradiction in line 241+244. Dev cannot mechanically implement both. (NEW-2, was OBJ-3 residual) Technical Hints line 277 specifies `start = max(0, ip_index) when ip_index is not None, else start = 0`. With K=3 (end = start+3), when ip_index is None the typed-lookup window is `canonical[0:3]` — scanning the first 3 canonical steps. AC-H3 line 241 lists 'no in_progress' as an orphan-triggering state that MUST NOT mutate, but with Case A active (subagent_type non-empty) the spec ALLOWS flipping any matching canonical step in indices 0-2. This is precisely the stale-state misflip OBJ-3 was supposed to prevent. (NEW-3, downstream-blocking) Canonical context-20260519-211515.json line 354 `acceptance_criteria_path: docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json` and line 355 `blast_radius_map_path: .claude/dev-registry/dev-20260519-211515/blast-radius-map.json` BOTH point to artifacts that belong to the parallel collision cycle (Cycle 2 of spec-20260518-225715), NOT D+H. Direct read of the AC file confirms it lists Cycle 2 ACs with codex_findings_incorporated referring to P1.1/P1.2/P2.4/P3.6/P3.8/P3.9 — none related to D or H. Downstream Dev/test-writer/QA following these paths will validate the WRONG ACs. Additionally, iteration_log.files_modified_iter1 and collision_report.preserved_artifact_paths still reference -DH suffix paths (docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515-DH.md, etc.) that no longer exist on disk after orchestrator promotion. Per Anti-Fraud Principle 5 (never substitute related-but-different verification), my preliminary PASS draft was incomplete: I verified the new Case A/Case B paragraphs in isolation but did not cross-check them against AC-H3's GIVEN/THEN wording or against the K=3 boundary case where ip_index=None. The codex adversarial review (3 findings, 2 BLOCKER + 1 MAJOR, all with PROPOSED_FIX) caught the omissions.

evidence_quality.notes: BA iter1 line-citations all verified end-to-end. The 6 production callers in AC-D3 each match grep results (orchestrator-gate.py:184, subagent-enforce.py:214, write-guard.sh:39, aggregate-check.py:389, read-size-guard.py:72, block-enterworktree.sh:48). Codex consultation succeeded via tee-less channel, returning 3 concrete findings each with a PROPOSED_FIX.

scope_alignment.notes: BA iter1 did not narrow or expand scope. D+H remain the only fix items; A/B/C/E/F/G remain out per Codex triage.

investigation_completeness.notes: BA iter1 added an iteration_log[] entry summarizing the 3 OBJ resolutions; component_chain[] expanded with the 6 production callers + 2 NOT-A-CALL-SITE labels; affected_files unchanged at 2 hooks + 1 test file.

affected_file_accuracy.notes: OBJ-1 RESOLVED. AC-D3 now enumerates exactly the 6 production callers with line citations and explicitly excludes the test file and lib definition from the target list. context JSON `production_callers_count: 6` and explicit `production_callers[]` list. component_chain reflects the corrected list. The 'all_occurrences' header at ticket line 102 reads '6 production callers + 1 test file with 3 calls + 1 lib definition'.

spec_text_vs_execution_drift.notes: TWO residual drifts in OBJ-2/OBJ-3 territory remain after iter1, BOTH introduced by the iter1 revision text: (a) AC-H3 (line 241) lists 'missing subagent_type in stdin' as an orphan-triggering GIVEN with general THEN 'subagent_calls UNCHANGED' (line 244), but Technical Hints Case B (line 286) says missing subagent_type falls through to legacy which CAN write if it resolves a valid target — direct contradiction. (b) Technical Hints line 277 admits `start = 0` when `ip_index is None`, but AC-H3 line 241 lists 'no in_progress' as orphan-MUST-NOT-mutate — Case A WILL flip matching canonical[0:3] when ip_index is None and subagent_type is non-empty, contradicting AC-H3. Both drifts make AC-H3 mechanically un-implementable: dev cannot satisfy both the AC text and the Technical Hints text simultaneously.

objections.claim: AC-H3 (ticket-20260519-211515.md:240-246) treats 'missing subagent_type in stdin' as an orphan-triggering condition with general THEN clause (line 244) requiring `subagent_calls` to remain UNCHANGED in shape (no keys added, no keys removed, no values flipped). Technical Hints Case B (line 286) says when subagent_type is absent, the legacy `_current_in_progress_index` + `_step_has_subagent_call` chain runs and CAN write `subagent_calls[str(ip_index)] = True` if the chain resolves a valid target. AC-H3 line 246 partially qualifies (writes blocked only when legacy ALSO returns None), but lines 241 + 244 in combination still state the broader orphan rule.
objections.problem: Internal contradiction: AC-H3 wording at GIVEN/THEN level says 'missing subagent_type ⇒ no mutation'; Case B says 'missing subagent_type ⇒ legacy chain MAY write if valid target'. Dev cannot mechanically satisfy both. The literal AC text is what QA will test against per `ac_alignment` schema. The Case B legacy-write path IS the AC-H2 serial-dispatch case (TodoWrite first, then Agent later) — that path MUST work, but AC-H3's general wording forbids it when subagent_type is absent.
objections.required_evidence: BA must revise AC-H3 to remove 'missing subagent_type in stdin' from the GIVEN list at line 241, OR explicitly split AC-H3 into two sub-ACs: (H3a) `subagent_type` absent + legacy resolves valid target ⇒ legacy write allowed (this is AC-H2-aligned behavior); (H3b) `subagent_type` absent + legacy returns None ⇒ no-write, no None key. The current line 246 partial qualifier is not sufficient because the GIVEN clause at line 241 still implies 'missing subagent_type' alone triggers the orphan-no-mutate THEN at line 244.

objections.claim: Technical Hints H step 1 helper specification (ticket-20260519-211515.md:276-279) says `_find_earliest_unflipped_matching_step` computes `start = max(0, ip_index)` when `ip_index is not None`, else `start = 0`. With K=3 (end = start + 3, line 278), when `ip_index is None` the window becomes `canonical[0:3]` — scanning the first 3 canonical steps for a typed match.
objections.problem: AC-H3 line 241 explicitly lists 'no in_progress' as an orphan-triggering bookmark state that MUST NOT mutate `subagent_calls`. Yet Case A is active whenever `subagent_type` is non-empty (line 283), regardless of whether `ip_index` is None. The combination ⇒ Case A may flip canonical[0:3] when ip_index is None and subagent_type is non-empty, directly contradicting AC-H3. Worse, this is the exact stale-state misflip class that OBJ-3 (and Codex's preferred path (a)) was supposed to prevent: at session restart with no in_progress, an Agent(subagent_type='qa') dispatch intended for some Step N could be applied to canonical[0]/[1]/[2] if any of those have matching subagent_call metadata.
objections.required_evidence: BA must revise the helper specification at line 277 to require a valid single anchor before Case A scans, e.g., remove the `else: start = 0` branch and instead exit 0 with no write when `ip_index is None`. Alternatively, add an explicit AC-H3 sub-case: 'GIVEN no in_progress step in bookmark AND `subagent_type` is non-empty, WHEN PostToolUse:Agent fires, THEN the hook exits 0 with no write — Case A is gated on a single valid in_progress anchor.' AC-H3 should also explicitly cover 'multiple in_progress' since `_current_in_progress_index` returns one of them by some unspecified rule. Codex's PROPOSED_FIX: 'Require a valid single anchor before Case A: exactly one in_progress index, else exit 0 no-write. Remove ip_index None → start=0, or explicitly add another deterministic target source if no-anchor parallel dispatch must be supported. Add AC-H3 tests for no-in-progress and multiple-in-progress.'

objections.claim: context-20260519-211515.json line 354 `acceptance_criteria_path: docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json` and line 355 `blast_radius_map_path: .claude/dev-registry/dev-20260519-211515/blast-radius-map.json` both point to artifacts that exist on disk but contain content for the parallel collision cycle (Cycle 2 of spec-20260518-225715), NOT D+H. Direct read of acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json confirms: line 4 `cycle: 2`, line 5 `spec: docs/dev/specs/spec-20260518-225715.md`, line 7 `generated_at: 2026-05-20T04:55:00Z` (same minute as collision), codex_findings_incorporated lists P1.1/P1.2/P2.4/P3.6/P3.8/P3.9 — all parallel-cycle items, none D or H. Additionally, iteration_log.files_modified_iter1[] (context line 389-390) and collision_report.preserved_artifact_paths[] (context line 459-460) still reference `-DH` suffixed paths (docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515-DH.md, docs/dev/context-20260519-211515-DH.json) that no longer exist on disk after the orchestrator promoted them to canonical paths.
objections.problem: Downstream consumers (Dev, test-writer, downstream QA) following `acceptance_criteria_path` will load Cycle 2's ACs, not D+H's. Downstream blast-radius checks will look at the wrong map. The stale `-DH` references mislead any human auditor reading collision_report or iteration_log. None of this risks the D+H code change itself, but it can cause downstream agents to verify the WRONG AC set or report the WRONG artifact set as the cycle's outputs.
objections.required_evidence: BA must either (a) regenerate `acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json` from D+H ACs (AC-D1..D4, AC-H1..H4, AC-D-H) and rewrite the blast-radius map for the 2 hooks + 1 test file scope, OR (b) move the colliding parallel-cycle artifacts to a non-conflicting path (e.g., `-CYCLE2` suffix mirroring the orchestrator's ticket/context handling) and let BA's D+H artifacts take the canonical AC/blast-radius slots. Additionally, BA must update iteration_log.files_modified_iter1[] and collision_report.preserved_artifact_paths[] to reflect the post-orchestrator-promotion state (no more `-DH` suffix on disk) — or remove these stale-state entries entirely. NOTE: this is largely an orchestrator-side cleanup gap (the orchestrator promoted ticket+context but did not migrate the AC/blast-radius companion artifacts); QA documents it here so BA iter2 / the orchestrator can resolve before Dev consumes the spec.

OBJ-1.notes: AC-D3 now enumerates exactly the 6 production callers with line citations (ticket lines 205-216). component_chain[] in context JSON contains 6 PRODUCTION-CALLER entries + 2 NOT-A-CALL-SITE entries. context JSON production_callers_count=6 and explicit production_callers[] list. Header at line 102 reads '6 production callers + 1 test file with 3 calls + 1 lib definition'. Non-callers explicitly excluded from AC-D3 target list at lines 112-114.

OBJ-2.notes: Case A and Case B are textually split as mutually exclusive (good). Explicit None-key guard 'target is not None and isinstance(target, int) before _record_subagent_call_legacy' present at line 287. AC-H3 line 245 explicitly forbids None-key write and cross-type wrong-step flip when subagent_type is non-empty. HOWEVER residual drift: AC-H3 line 241 GIVEN clause includes 'missing subagent_type in stdin' alongside other orphan triggers, with general THEN at line 244 'subagent_calls UNCHANGED' — this contradicts Case B's legacy-write behavior at line 286. See new objection OBJ-4.

OBJ-3.notes: K=3 bounded window present (line 278). Rationale recorded in Edge Cases & Risks (line 183). HOWEVER residual drift: Technical Hints line 277 admits `start = 0` when `ip_index is None`, opening the typed-lookup window to `canonical[0:3]` — exactly the kind of stale-state misflip OBJ-3 was supposed to prevent. AC-H3 line 241 lists 'no in_progress' as orphan-MUST-NOT-mutate, contradicting Case A's behavior in that state. See new objection OBJ-5.

OBJ-1_six_callers_enumeration.details: AC-D3 mechanically reproducible: enumerates 6 callers with exact line citations matching grep results. Non-callers (3 test invocations + 1 lib definition) explicitly excluded from target list and labeled NOT a call site.

OBJ-2_case_a_case_b_separation.details: Technical Hints text splits Case A and Case B as mutually exclusive with explicit no-fall-through language. None-key guard mandated before writer. AC-H3 line 245 covers Case A miss + non-empty subagent_type case. RESIDUAL: line 241 GIVEN clause still lists 'missing subagent_type' as an orphan trigger, contradicting Case B's legacy-write path. See OBJ-4.

OBJ-3_k3_bounded_window.details: K=3 window present with bounds-rationale recorded. RESIDUAL: ip_index=None fallback to start=0 admits stale-state misflip into canonical[0:3]. See OBJ-5.

verification_method.details: Read canonical files in full: ticket-20260519-211515.md (389 lines), context-20260519-211515.json (470 lines), ba-qa-report-20260519-211515.json (102 lines). Cross-referenced AC text against Technical Hints text. Listed -DH file paths on disk (confirmed missing post-promotion). Read acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json header (confirmed Cycle 2 content, not D+H). Invoked codex adversarial review via tee-less channel; codex returned 3 findings (2 BLOCKER + 1 MAJOR), all incorporated as OBJ-4, OBJ-5, OBJ-6.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Companion AC file at the canonical task-id slot contains Cycle 2 of spec-20260518-225715 content, not D+H. This is the orchestrator-side collision-resolution gap (orchestrator promoted ticket+context but did not migrate AC/blast-radius companion artifacts). Surfaced as OBJ-6 to ensure BA iter2 / the orchestrator resolves before Dev consumes the spec.
Same as above: blast-radius map at the canonical task-id slot contains Cycle 2 content, not D+H. Same orchestrator-side gap.

Root cause of the collision: two BA cycles received the same task-id 20260519-211515 from the orchestrator's task-id allocator, likely a clock-second collision or missing distinctness check. This is out of scope for the current cycle's D+H fix but warrants a separate orchestrator-cleanup ticket. (Already noted in BA spec collision_report.orchestrator_required_resolution[4].)

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 3 findings via tee-less invocation, all with PROPOSED_FIX: (1) BLOCKER — AC-H3 contradicts Case B for missing subagent_type (AC-H3 line 241+244 says no-mutation but Case B at line 286 allows legacy write); (2) BLOCKER — K=3 window unsafe when ip_index is None or non-unique (line 277 `else start=0` admits canonical[0:3] misflip, contradicting AC-H3 line 241 'no in_progress must not mutate'); (3) MAJOR — canonical context references stale collision artifacts (acceptance_criteria_path and blast_radius_map_path point to Cycle 2 content; iteration_log and collision_report reference -DH paths that no longer exist after orchestrator promotion).
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 3 codex findings are real spec defects, verified by direct file-read. Classified as in_scope_real_bug per codex skill Rule 3. Incorporated as OBJ-4 (BLOCKER, from codex finding 1), OBJ-5 (BLOCKER, from codex finding 2), OBJ-6 (MAJOR, from codex finding 3). Without codex's adversarial challenge, my preliminary PASS draft would have shipped — I had only checked the Case A/Case B paragraphs in isolation, not cross-checked them against AC-H3 GIVEN/THEN wording or boundary-case behavior (ip_index=None, no in_progress, multiple in_progress). Per Anti-Fraud Principle 5 (never substitute related-but-different verification), this was incomplete verification on my part; codex caught the omissions.
